recipe.title: Thursday: Teriyaki Salmon Rice Bowls
ingredients: cooked jasmine or short-grain white rice (about 1¼ cups dry)
2¼ lbs skinless salmon fillets, cut into 1-inch cubes
steps: Rinse rice; cook with 2¼ cups water + pinch of salt. Bring to boil, reduce to low, cover, simmer 15 minutes. Rest 5 minutes, fluff.
Pat salmon dry; season with salt and pepper.
Whisk soy sauce, honey, rice vinegar, brown sugar, sesame oil, garlic, ginger in a small bowl.
Heat olive oil in large skillet over medium-high. Sear salmon 2–3 minutes per side until golden.
Pour teriyaki sauce over salmon. Simmer. Stir cornstarch slurry; drizzle in. Stir 1–2 minutes until thickened and glossy. Remove from heat.
**Assemble bowls:** rice on the bottom, salmon + sauce, then arrange broccoli, cucumber, avocado, carrots, edamame around it. Garnish with green onions and sesame seeds.
